title: Present time
description: Learn how English present tenses are formed and used.

topics.title: Present simple
topics.description: Used for habits, permanent situations, and general truths.

uses.usage: Talking about how often things happen
uses.example: Angela doesn't visit us very often.
Carlo works in a travel agent's.
Do you have an up-to-date passport?
Poland is in the European Union.

notes.content: We can also use do or does in an affirmative present simple sentence to add emphasis.
examples: “You don't like going by bus, do you?”
“Actually, I do like going by bus for short distances.”
The bus isn't quicker than the train, but it does stop right outside the factory.

topics.title: Present continuous
topics.description: Used for actions happening now and for temporary or changing situations.

uses.example: Mike is driving to work at the moment.
Taxi drivers aren't stopping at the train station because of the roadworks.
Are they staying in a hotel near the Olympic stadium?
Holidays abroad are becoming increasingly popular.
Dad is always cleaning the car when I want to use it!

topics.title: Present perfect simple
topics.description: Used for past actions or states that are connected to the present.

uses.usage: Situations and states that started in the past and are still true
uses.example: She's had her motorbike for over six years.

uses.usage: A series of actions continuing up to now
uses.example: We've travelled by taxi, bus, plane and train – all in the last twenty-four hours!

uses.usage: Completed actions at an unspecified time in the past
uses.example: Have you ever flown in a helicopter?

uses.usage: Completed actions where the present result is important
